title: Prysm consensus-client bug after Fusaka upgrade caused validator outages, missed epochs and ~382 ETH in lost rewards; fixes deployed and network recovered
description: A Prysm client bug that appeared immediately after Ethereum's Fusaka upgrade (epoch 411392, Dec 4, 2025, 21:49 UTC) triggered expensive state recomputation from certain attestations, exhausting resources and degrading Prysm validator performance. Validator participation for Prysm fell from >95% to ~75%, the network missed 41 epochs and about 382 ETH in proof rewards were lost. Prysm represented ~15%–22.71% of validators. Emergency runtime flags were used and permanent fixes shipped in v7.0.1 and v7.1.0. Client diversity (other clients kept ~75%–85% of validators) prevented finality loss and the network returned to ~99% participation within 24 hours. The Fusaka upgrade itself succeeded and added PeerDAS to increase blob capacity for Layer‑2 scaling.

title: Crypto winter sparks sell-off in public crypto-treasury companies — Strategy (MSTR) down ~40% as mNAV nears 1x; Strategy discloses $1.44B cash reserve
description: After Bitcoin's October crash, more than 180 public companies with crypto treasuries saw share-price declines: Strategy (MSTR) fell ~40% and peers (NAKA, ABTC, BRR) dropped 39–65%. Ether-holding treasuries also slid (BMNR, SBET, BTBT). Investors are watching mNAV (market cap vs. crypto holdings); Strategy’s mNAV approached 1x, raising concerns it might sell crypto to meet dividends/debt. Strategy announced a $1.44B cash reserve to cover payouts for 21 months and its CEO defended the company model and valuation.

title: Bank of Japan 25 bps hike (98% priced) could trigger 20–30% Bitcoin drop as prior BoJ moves coincided with >20% BTC falls
description: Bitcoin is under heavy selling pressure ahead of the Bank of Japan decision, with Polymarket pricing a 98% chance of a 5 basis-point BoJ hike. BTC trades near $90,000 and technical indicators (daily death cross, bearish flag, price below Ichimoku cloud and Supertrend) signal further downside. Near-term support is the November low around $80,000; a break could open a deeper drop to roughly $74,500–$75,000. The piece also cites Fed guidance for only one 2026 cut and macro divergence that could unwind carry trades.

title: Coinbase XRP open interest rises while price stalls — Will the $2 line hold or crack under pressure?
description: Open interest for XRP on Coinbase has increased even as XRP’s spot price is flat-to-down, a divergence that often reflects new leveraged positions and potential for higher volatility. Technically XRP remains in a wider declining structure (lower highs, failed mid-range recovery since October) with weak momentum and neutral-to-bearish RSI, so a sustained breakout requires fresh spot demand or directional conviction. Current metrics show more long liquidations than shorts and funding/long–short ratios are not strongly bullish, suggesting this is early positioning that could become a leverage trap unless follow-through appears.

title: Cardano-linked privacy token Midnight (NIGHT) posts >$1B 24‑hour volume and market cap; Hoskinson calls it “absolutely remarkable”
description: Midnight (NIGHT), a Cardano‑linked privacy token, began active trading and registered over $1 billion in 24‑hour volume and market capitalization, with a volume-to-market-cap ratio of ~96.5%. NIGHT traded around $0.069, briefly touched $0.07, then consolidated between $0.066–$0.071. Bybit accounted for >$650M of turnover; Binance, Alpha, OKX, KuCoin and Gate also contributed. Project describes itself as a programmable privacy network using zero‑knowledge proofs, dual‑ledger architecture and selective disclosure tooling.

title: Bitcoin bounces above $90k after cost-basis support near $80k; analysts warn BoJ rate hike could push BTC below $70k
description: Bitcoin rebounded to above $90,000 after finding confluence support around $80,000 from three cost-basis measures: the True Market Mean (~$81,000), the U.S. spot ETF cost basis (~$83,844 per Glassnode) and the 2024 yearly VWAP cost basis (~$83,000 per Checkonchain). These on-chain and ETF-weighted metrics acted as support during the pullback, indicating concentrated buyer demand and conviction in the $80k region.

title: CoinDesk names President Trump most influential in 2025 as policymakers dominate list; executive orders, congressional pressure and family crypto ties shape policy amid Senate markup uncertainty
description: CoinDesk's 2025 Most Influential list ranks U.S. President Donald Trump as the top individual, citing his executive orders, pressure on Congress and crypto-linked business activity tied to his family. The piece highlights policymakers — especially U.S. lawmakers — as central drivers of crypto narrative and policy, and reports uncertainty over upcoming Senate markup hearings for market-structure legislation, sticking points tied to provisions related to Trump’s family crypto interests, and pending votes on regulatory nominees.

title: December rebound shows volatility: U.S. spot Bitcoin ETFs +$198M, Ether ETFs +$143M; ETH ETFs record $19.4M outflows on Dec 12
description: In December 2025 U.S. spot Bitcoin and Ethereum ETFs logged combined inflows of roughly $341M — $198M to Bitcoin ETFs and $143.35M to Ethereum ETFs. BlackRock’s IBIT added nearly $4B in holdings over 13 days (from $66.26B to $70.12B); ETHA led Ethereum inflows. ETF-held Bitcoin rose above $118B (about 6.57% of BTC market cap). The flows represent a rebound after steep November outflows ($3.48B from Bitcoin ETFs and $1.42B from Ethereum ETFs) following the Oct. 10–11 flash crash and weak crypto performance.

title: VivoPower and Lean Ventures' $300M Ripple fund goes live in South Korea, offers XRP exposure to institutional and retail investors
description: VivoPower received Ripple’s approval to launch a $300 million institutional fund via a joint venture with Seoul-based Lean Ventures that will purchase Ripple Labs preferred shares to give institutions equity exposure instead of XRP tokens. VivoPower’s digital arm, Vivo Federation, will source and buy the shares. The fund targets South Korean demand, cites recent regulatory progress including an OCC banking license for Ripple, and is expected to generate about $75 million in management and performance fees over three years. VivoPower stock rose about 13% on the announcement.

title: Coinbase: Fed 'light QE' and T‑bill reserve buys should boost liquidity into April 2026, but on‑chain and options flows show persistent selling pressure on Bitcoin
description: Coinbase Institutional says the Fed’s 25bp cut to 3.5%–3.75% and announced reserve management purchases (U.S. T‑Bills) amount to a near‑term liquidity boost — forecasting reserve growth likely to continue until April 2026 and calling the shift “light QE.” Fed funds futures imply two 2026 cuts (50bps). Bitwise CEO Forecasts a strong 2026, saying market conditions are aligning for a big year. Offsetting this, Glassnode describes markets in a “mild bearish phase” after an October liquidation erased about $19B in leveraged bets; Bitcoin is trading near the lower end of its range and large‑holder selling pressure persists.

title: OKX accuses Mantra of misleading OM holders, seizes accounts and triggers legal fights over OM migration and custody
description: OKX alleges Mantra spread a misleading narrative about OM and that multiple connected accounts used large quantities of OM as collateral to borrow USDT, artificially inflating OM’s price before a collapse. OKX says its risk team flagged the activity, requested corrections, and — after account holders refused — took control of the related accounts; only a small portion of OM was liquidated and losses were absorbed by the OKX Security Fund. Third‑party analysis cited by OKX indicates the sharp price drop was largely driven by perpetual trading outside OKX. Mantra CEO JP Mullin urged OM holders to withdraw tokens from OKX, disputed OKX’s proposed migration timeline (stating ERC‑20 OM cannot be migrated before deprecation on Jan 15, 2026), and confirmed a protocol‑level redenomination (1:4) requiring no user action. OKX sent a Dec. 10 letter rebutting Mullin’s public comments, has submitted evidence to regulators, and multiple legal proceedings are now underway, leaving migration details and exchange custody of OM unclear for holders.

title: Cardano (ADA) argued to have more upside than Bitcoin (BTC) as ETF-driven demand limits BTC's near-term gains

title: Prysm consensus-client bug after Fusaka upgrade caused validator outages, missed epochs and ~382 ETH in lost rewards; fixes deployed and network recovered
description: A Prysm client bug that appeared immediately after Ethereum's Fusaka upgrade (epoch 411392, Dec 4, 2025, 21:49 UTC) triggered expensive state recomputation from certain attestations, exhausting resources and degrading Prysm validator performance. Validator participation for Prysm fell from >95% to ~75%, the network missed 41 epochs and about 382 ETH in proof rewards were lost. Prysm represented ~15%–22.71% of validators. Emergency runtime flags were used and permanent fixes shipped in v7.0.1 and v7.1.0. Client diversity (other clients kept ~75%–85% of validators) prevented finality loss and the network returned to ~99% participation within 24 hours. The Fusaka upgrade itself succeeded and added PeerDAS to increase blob capacity for Layer‑2 scaling.

title: Gulf oil-linked sovereign funds and family offices are boosting Bitcoin liquidity through spot-ETF inflows, with Abu Dhabi emerging as a hub
description: In 2025, sovereign wealth funds, family offices and private banking networks tied to Gulf oil revenues are supplying meaningful Bitcoin liquidity by investing through regulated channels—primarily spot Bitcoin ETFs. Abu Dhabi has become a focal point, supported by the Abu Dhabi Global Market and rising local asset-management activity. These inflows can deepen order books, narrow bid-ask spreads, and increase ETF creations/redemptions and hedging activity, strengthening spot and derivatives market structure. The piece also warns of limits: large institutional allocations can exit quickly (e.g., sizable IBIT outflows), and regulatory shifts or market volatility could reverse liquidity gains.

Strategy announced a $1.44B cash reserve to cover payouts for 21 months and its CEO defended the company model and valuation.
text: Crypto winter sparks sell-off in public crypto-treasury companies — Strategy (MSTR) down ~40% as mNAV nears 1x; Strategy discloses $1.44B cash reserve. After Bitcoin's October crash, more than 180 public companies with crypto treasuries saw share-price declines: Strategy (MSTR) fell ~40% and peers (NAKA, ABTC, BRR) dropped 39–65%. Ether-holding treasuries also slid (BMNR, SBET, BTBT). Investors are watching mNAV (market cap vs. crypto holdings); Strategy’s mNAV approached 1x, raising concerns it might sell crypto to meet dividends/debt. Strategy announced a $1.44B cash reserve to cover payouts for 21 months and its CEO defended the company model and valuation.

title: Institutions keep building Bitcoin infrastructure while sport-linked “SportFi” fan tokens (Chiliz, Polygon, Binance, Ethereum) become mainstream gateway to crypto
description: Even after a >25% Bitcoin decline in Oct–Nov 2025, institutions are increasingly adopting Bitcoin and building mainstream infrastructure. Mass adoption still tends to follow institutional endorsement, but broader retail uptake is spreading fastest through familiar gateways — previously GameFi, now sport-linked tokens (SportFi). Major clubs and sporting organizations have launched fan tokens on Chiliz, Binance, Polygon and Ethereum; daily trading volumes for these tokens have rivaled top market tokens and prices often move with match events and club performance. With ~3.5 billion football fans globally, sport-linked tokens could onboard millions of users who use football knowledge to engage with crypto markets, complementing institutional adoption.

title: Bank of Japan 25 bps hike (98% priced) could trigger 20–30% Bitcoin drop as prior BoJ moves coincided with >20% BTC falls
description: Bitcoin is under heavy selling pressure ahead of the Bank of Japan decision, with Polymarket pricing a 98% chance of a 5 basis-point BoJ hike. BTC trades near $90,000 and technical indicators (daily death cross, bearish flag, price below Ichimoku cloud and Supertrend) signal further downside. Near-term support is the November low around $80,000; a break could open a deeper drop to roughly $74,500–$75,000. The piece also cites Fed guidance for only one 2026 cut and macro divergence that could unwind carry trades.

title: Coinbase XRP open interest rises while price stalls — Will the $2 line hold or crack under pressure?
description: Open interest for XRP on Coinbase has increased even as XRP’s spot price is flat-to-down, a divergence that often reflects new leveraged positions and potential for higher volatility. Technically XRP remains in a wider declining structure (lower highs, failed mid-range recovery since October) with weak momentum and neutral-to-bearish RSI, so a sustained breakout requires fresh spot demand or directional conviction. Current metrics show more long liquidations than shorts and funding/long–short ratios are not strongly bullish, suggesting this is early positioning that could become a leverage trap unless follow-through appears.
